title: Coding Challenge
description: Generate a practical coding exercise from topic, difficulty, and available time.

placeholders.constraints: For example: no external state library, mobile-first, include retry handling.

frontend.beginner: Build a task panel with clear UI state
frontend.intermediate: Implement an extensible form workflow interface
frontend.advanced: Design a collaborative UI with offline replay support

backend.beginner: Build a small but reliable REST task API
backend.intermediate: Design a notification service with retry and rate limits
backend.advanced: Build a multi-tenant async job system with idempotent writes

algorithm.beginner: Implement an interval merger with edge-case validation
algorithm.intermediate: Design a priority-aware task scheduling algorithm
algorithm.advanced: Implement a high-throughput cache eviction simulator

summaries.frontend: Use {{minutes}} minutes to ship a page with sound state handling and complete interaction details.
summaries.backend: Use {{minutes}} minutes to define APIs, core data flow, and failure handling with clear reasoning.
summaries.algorithm: Use {{minutes}} minutes to build a working solution and explain complexity, edge cases, and optimizations.

frontend.0: Sketch the page structure and core state first, then connect the primary user flow.
frontend.1: Add loading, empty, error, and validation states instead of shipping only the happy path.
frontend.2: Close with reusable components and explain why the split makes sense.

backend.0: Clarify the resource model, key APIs, and state transitions first.
backend.1: Handle idempotency, retries, permission boundaries, and logs.
backend.2: Explain what breaks first if traffic grows by 10x.

algorithm.0: Start with a correct baseline before reaching for optimization.
algorithm.1: Call out complexity, extreme inputs, and degradation paths.
algorithm.2: Explain how you would evolve the solution if constraints tightened.

beginner.0: The implementation is complete, clearly named, and usable end to end.
beginner.1: Basic edge cases are covered rather than only sample input.
beginner.2: The solution can be explained in simple, concrete language.

intermediate.0: The structure is clean and appropriately abstracted.
intermediate.1: Error handling and state transitions show engineering discipline.
intermediate.2: Alternative options and tradeoffs can be explained.

advanced.0: The solution balances design quality, performance, and extensibility.
advanced.1: Risks are surfaced with a progressive evolution path.
advanced.2: There is a clear answer for changing constraints, not just a static solution.

0.title: Scope fast
0.description: Set the delivery boundary early so {{difficulty}} work does not drift.

1.title: Finish the core path
1.description: Get the main flow working before polishing support cases.

2.title: Patch edges
2.description: Handle validation, failure states, and recovery paths.

3.title: Review and explain
3.description: Use the last minutes to summarize tradeoffs, risks, and next steps.
